cost_sensitivity.cost_model: Future diagnostics must reject interpretation if any weekday-conditioned readout only survives before fees, slippage, and spread assumptions.
cost_sensitivity.scope: Value-free migration metadata only; no execution cost estimate is produced.

cost_assumptions.scope: research planning only; no execution or tradability claim

data_assumptions.data_dependency_detail: Fully covered by the existing SessionFeatureName.DAY_OF_WEEK feature plus existing RTH/session-position features; no new FeatureRequest, no exchange-calendar extension, and no external feed required. Lowest data dependency in the entire differentiated-substrate set.
data_assumptions.missing_feature_candidates: No new feature candidates are declared by this legacy card.
data_assumptions.missing_label_candidates: No new label candidates are declared by this legacy card.

exclusion_rules: Use the existing point-in-time day_of_week feature directly; condition jointly on the existing rth_segment_flag / session_id so holiday and early-close sessions are not silently pooled with full sessions. No new metadata surface is required.
Do not test needs_paid_data records in V0; paid-data sourcing is RED-deferred.
Do not edit or delete the legacy Track-A card until a later retirement phase is authorized.
expected_failure_modes: A pure calendar position is structurally independent of the realized-price/quote main effects the dead mechanisms (regime, vwap_session, bbo_tradability, liquidity_pa) conditioned on. This is the WEAKEST orthogonality claim in the seasonality set because day_of_week is the most generic calendar conditioner and is most at risk of being a proxy for an already-present session-position effect rather than a distinct flow mechanism; the diagnostic, not this statement, decides.

Residualize forward outcomes against the dead-mechanism factors (regime/vwap_session/bbo_tradability/liquidity_pa) AND against intraday session position (minutes_from_rth_open) before scoring the day-of-week conditioner, to confirm it is not a relabeling of an intraday-position effect already captured elsewhere.

Fully covered by the existing SessionFeatureName.DAY_OF_WEEK feature plus existing RTH/session-position features; no new FeatureRequest, no exchange-calendar extension, and no external feed required. Lowest data dependency in the entire differentiated-substrate set.
promotion_criteria: Any future WATCH or CANDIDATE routing requires reviewer-gated evidence and must preserve EXPLORATORY promotion guards.
Any future diagnostic must pass no-lookahead, duplicate-exposure, and testability checks before interpretation.

timestamp_assumptions.lookahead_description: Day-of-week is deterministic from the bar timestamp (the existing day_of_week feature is computed from bar_start_ts.weekday()) and is fully known-ahead, so the conditioner carries no lookahead hazard. The only hazard is treating a holiday-shortened or early-close session as an ordinary weekday; that must be segmented with the existing session-type classification, not a naive weekday rule.
timestamp_assumptions.lookahead_level: low lookahead risk declared by the legacy Track-A card
timestamp_assumptions.post_event_knowable_features: The legacy card declares no post-event knowable feature list.

hypothesis_card.author: ALPHA_IDEA_TO_VERDICT_LOOP_V0/IVL-P01
hypothesis_card.expected_mechanism: We test whether intraday forward behavior conditioned on the known-ahead calendar day-of-week (and the interaction of day-of-week with intraday session position) differs systematically across weekdays. This is a falsifiable seasonality-conditioning hypothesis, not a profitability claim.
falsification_criteria: Reject or requeue this migrated record if required governed features or labels are unavailable before diagnostics.
Block any later verdict if no-lookahead controls or duplicate exposure checks fail before a metric is inspected.
hypothesis_card.rationale: Calendar-driven flow can cluster by weekday for structural reasons (weekly fund-flow cadence, Monday gap absorption, Friday position-squaring into the weekend); whether any of this produces a measurable conditional forward pattern is an empirical question the diagnostics resolve.
risks: Day-of-week is deterministic from the bar timestamp (the existing day_of_week feature is computed from bar_start_ts.weekday()) and is fully known-ahead, so the conditioner carries no lookahead hazard. The only hazard is treating a holiday-shortened or early-close session as an ordinary weekday; that must be segmented with the existing session-type classification, not a naive weekday rule.

A pure calendar position is structurally independent of the realized-price/quote main effects the dead mechanisms (regime, vwap_session, bbo_tradability, liquidity_pa) conditioned on. This is the WEAKEST orthogonality claim in the seasonality set because day_of_week is the most generic calendar conditioner and is most at risk of being a proxy for an already-present session-position effect rather than a distinct flow mechanism; the diagnostic, not this statement, decides.

Fully covered by the existing SessionFeatureName.DAY_OF_WEEK feature plus existing RTH/session-position features; no new FeatureRequest, no exchange-calendar extension, and no external feed required. Lowest data dependency in the entire differentiated-substrate set.
